tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low total CO₂, significant proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function with possible tubular involvement. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate urinary tract pathology. Low HbA1c with normoglycemia suggests recent changes in glucose metabolism. Recommend further renal assessment and clinical correlation for possible acute or chronic kidney disease and urinary tract issues.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
